title: radermachera quadripinnata
intro: Radermachera quadripinnata, also known as the Mallard tree, is a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its attractive white flowers and quadrifoliate leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Radermachera quadripinnata include reducing fever, treating skin infections, and aiding in the management of respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with its leaves and flowers being commonly used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
